items.title: Thousands stranded after U.S.-Israeli strikes close Middle East airspace
items.standfirst: Thousands of travelers are stranded after the U.S.-Israeli operations in Iran and retaliatory strikes closed airspace across the Middle East. CBS News' Shanelle Kaul has more.
items.content: Thousands of travelers are stranded after the U.S.-Israeli operations in Iran and retaliatory strikes closed airspace across the Middle East. CBS News' Shanelle Kaul has more.

items.title: Inside look at damage in Israel after Iranian retaliatory strikes
items.standfirst: Iran is launching retaliatory strikes across the Middle East in response to the U.S.-Israeli operations against the Iranian regime. CBS News chief correspondent Matt Gutman has a look at some of the damage in Israel from a missile strike.
items.content: Iran is launching retaliatory strikes across the Middle East in response to the U.S.-Israeli operations against the Iranian regime. CBS News chief correspondent Matt Gutman has a look at some of the damage in Israel from a missile strike.

items.title: In maps: Strikes across Iran and the Middle East

items.content: Iranian strikes on targets across the Middle East and Gulf regions continued on Monday after the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran killing the country's supreme leader.

items.title: What happens now with the Iranian regime?
items.standfirst: Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei has been killed along with 49 senior regime leaders, according to the White House, in the U.S.-Israeli strikes against Iran. That leaves the future of the regime in question. Abba Milani, director of the Iranian Studies Program at Stanford University, joins CBS News to discuss the regime and the war.
items.content: Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei has been killed along with 49 senior regime leaders, according to the White House, in the U.S.-Israeli strikes against Iran. That leaves the future of the regime in question. Abba Milani, director of the Iranian Studies Program at Stanford University, joins CBS News to discuss the regime and the war.

items.title: Several killed in Israel after Iran launches retaliatory strikes; Trump gives update on war
items.standfirst: At least 11 people have been killed in Israel as Iran launches retaliatory strikes against countries across the Persian Gulf. CBS News chief correspondent Matt Gutman reports from Tel Aviv and CBS News chief White House correspondent Nancy Cordes has the latest on what President Trump is saying about the war.
items.content: At least 11 people have been killed in Israel as Iran launches retaliatory strikes against countries across the Persian Gulf. items.title: "Sense of déjà vu" in Lebanon as Middle East conflict widens
items.standfirst: The Israeli military said on March 2nd it hit more than 70 Hezbollah targets including missile launchers in a wave of strikes against the Iran-backed armed group in southern Lebanon. This comes as Lebanon bans the militant group's military activities. FRANCE 24's Renée Davis has more from Beirut.
items.content: The Israeli military said on March 2nd it hit more than 70 Hezbollah targets including missile launchers in a wave of strikes against the Iran-backed armed group in southern Lebanon. This comes as Lebanon bans the militant group's military activities. FRANCE 24's Renée Davis has more from Beirut.

items.title: Lebanon bans Hezbollah military activities as Middle East war spreads
items.standfirst: The Lebanese government has imposed an "immediate ban" on Hezbollah's military activities and called on the group to hand over its weapons to the state. The decision comes after Iran-backed Hezbollah launched rockets at Israel, provoking retaliatory Israeli strikes, which killed at least 31 people and injured 150 in Lebanon. Israel announced an offensive campaign and warned it would send Hezbollah chief Naim Qassem to "the bottom of hell" - like Iran's supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
items.content: The Lebanese government has imposed an "immediate ban" on Hezbollah's military activities and called on the group to hand over its weapons to the state. The decision comes after Iran-backed Hezbollah launched rockets at Israel, provoking retaliatory Israeli strikes, which killed at least 31 people and injured 150 in Lebanon. Israel announced an offensive campaign and warned it would send Hezbollah chief Naim Qassem to "the bottom of hell" - like Iran's supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

items.title: Pakistan deploys troops and imposes curfew after deadly Iran protests
items.standfirst: Pakistan on Monday deployed troops and imposed a three-day curfew in two northern cities following deadly protests sparked by the US-Israeli strikes that killed Iran's supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Several people were killed in violent protests that targeted UN buildings and US diplomatic missions.
items.content: Pakistan on Monday deployed troops and imposed a three-day curfew in two northern cities following deadly protests sparked by the US-Israeli strikes that killed Iran's supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Several people were killed in violent protests that targeted UN buildings and US diplomatic missions.

He also said the operation was ahead of schedule. FRANCE 24's Fraser Jackson tells us more from Washington.

items.title: 'Whatever it takes': Trump says the Iran conflict will continue
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump laid out a potential timeline of the Iran conflict saying, "we projected four to five weeks, but we have capability to go far longer than that." The president also said Iran had to be stopped from creating nuclear weapons and ballistic missiles for the safety of U.S. citizens.

items.title: What the war in Iran means for global energy security
items.standfirst: Qatar's state-run energy firm says it has halted Liquefied Natural Gas production following Iranian attacks on facilities at two of its main gas processing bases. This is only the latest consequence of the Middle East conflict on global markets. FRANCE 24's Environment Editor Valerie Dekimpe looks at the ripple effects of a war that's igniting fears of a major energy crisis.
items.content: Qatar's state-run energy firm says it has halted Liquefied Natural Gas production following Iranian attacks on facilities at two of its main gas processing bases. This is only the latest consequence of the Middle East conflict on global markets. FRANCE 24's Environment Editor Valerie Dekimpe looks at the ripple effects of a war that's igniting fears of a major energy crisis.

items.title: What is the strait of Hormuz and why is it crucial for oil supplies?
items.standfirst: Effective closure of the narrow waterway could spell trouble for many developed economies US-Israel war on Iran – latest updatesThe US-Israeli war on Iran has ignited fears that escalating military aggression in the Middle East could send oil prices soaring, push up prices at the pump and drive a global economic downturn.The US began “major combat operations” in Iran on Saturday morning, shortly after Israel launched a strike against Tehran. Within hours of the US-Israeli strikes, Iran’s Revolutionary Guards reportedly warned tankers in the strait of Hormuz that no ship would be allowed to pass through the world’s most critical oil trade route. Continue reading...
items.content: Effective closure of the narrow waterway could spell trouble for many developed economies US-Israel war on Iran – latest updatesThe US-Israeli war on Iran has ignited fears that escalating military aggression in the Middle East could send oil prices soaring, push up prices at the pump and drive a global economic downturn.The US began “major combat operations” in Iran on Saturday morning, shortly after Israel launched a strike against Tehran. Within hours of the US-Israeli strikes, Iran’s Revolutionary Guards reportedly warned tankers in the strait of Hormuz that no ship would be allowed to pass through the world’s most critical oil trade route. Continue reading...

items.title: ‘Duty to judge what is in Britain's national interest’: Starmer defends UK position on Iran
items.standfirst: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer has pushed back after criticism from US President Trump over Britain's decision to stay out of the initial US and Israeli strikes against Iran. It "is my duty to judge what is in Britain's national interest", Starmer told parliament on Monday.
items.content: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer has pushed back after criticism from US President Trump over Britain's decision to stay out of the initial US and Israeli strikes against Iran. It "is my duty to judge what is in Britain's national interest", Starmer told parliament on Monday.

items.title: "There is a universal feeling the UK shouldn't get involved in Iran"
items.standfirst: British Prime ​Minister Keir Starmer on ​March 2nd said he stood by his decision not ​to ‌support initial joint ⁠U.S.-Israeli strikes against Iran. Earlier, U.S. President Donald Trump had ⁠criticised his approach. But "there is a universal feeling the UK shouldn't be getting involved", says FRANCE 24's Philip Turle.
items.content: British Prime ​Minister Keir Starmer on ​March 2nd said he stood by his decision not ​to ‌support initial joint ⁠U.S.-Israeli strikes against Iran. Earlier, U.S. President Donald Trump had ⁠criticised his approach. But "there is a universal feeling the UK shouldn't be getting involved", says FRANCE 24's Philip Turle.

items.title: Daily Mail royal editor denies using private investigator to ‘blag’ Harry information
items.standfirst: Rebecca English shown emails at high court trial suggesting investigator ‘went out on a limb’ to help herThe Daily Mail’s royal editor has denied using a private investigator to “blag” information about the Duke of Sussex and his former girlfriends, as she was shown emails suggesting the investigator “went out on a limb” to help her.Rebecca English’s name appears on six of the articles cited by Prince Harry in his case against Associated Newspapers Ltd (ANL), publisher of the Daily Mail. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Why did AWS Middle East services fail? #tech
clusters.neutral_headline: Why did AWS Middle East services fail? #tech
bullet_summary: Attack, damage, and ripple effects Cloud operators reported physical strikes on one of their data center sites in the United Arab Emirates
Amazon said “objects” struck infrastructure inside a Middle East availability zone, producing sparks and fire that damaged equipment and disrupted services
The provider said the impacts hit an availability zone used by customers across the region…

items.title: What caused AWS outages in the UAE? #tech
items.standfirst: Physical damage to a data center amid regional strikes AWS reported that objects struck one of its Middle East data centers in the UAE, specifically affecting the Middle East (UAE) region availability zone. The provider said the impacts hit an availability zone used by customers across the region…
items.content: Physical damage to a data center amid regional strikes AWS reported that objects struck one of its Middle East data centers in the UAE, specifically affecting the Middle East (UAE) region availability zone. items.title: Amazon Cloud Disrupted After ‘Objects’ Hit UAE Data Center
items.standfirst: Amazon Web Services Inc. suffered a disruption to its services after unidentified objects struck one of its data centers in the United Arab Emirates, causing a fire.
items.content: Amazon Web Services Inc. suffered a disruption to its services after unidentified objects struck one of its data centers in the United Arab Emirates, causing a fire.

clusters.title: Latest details on deadly Austin bar shooting possibly linked to Iran war
clusters.neutral_headline: Deadly Texas bar shooting 'potentially act of terrorism', FBI says
bullet_summary: Two people were killed and 14 others injured, authorities said
Reported by CBS News, BBC World

items.title: Latest details on deadly Austin bar shooting possibly linked to Iran war
items.standfirst: A deadly shooting at a popular bar in Austin, Texas, is being investigated for possible links to terrorism. Two people were killed and 14 others injured, authorities said.
items.content: A deadly shooting at a popular bar in Austin, Texas, is being investigated for possible links to terrorism. Two people were killed and 14 others injured, authorities said.

items.title: Democrats make final push ahead of Texas primaries
items.standfirst: Texas voters are gearing up for the state's Tuesday midterm primaries following a deadly shooting in Austin that is being investigated for possible terrorism links. CBS News' Nidia Cavazos has more.
items.content: Texas voters are gearing up for the state's Tuesday midterm primaries following a deadly shooting in Austin that is being investigated for possible terrorism links. CBS News' Nidia Cavazos has more.

items.title: Deadly Texas bar shooting 'potentially act of terrorism', FBI says
items.standfirst: Two people were killed and 14 injured, with the suspected gunman, identified by some media outlets, also shot dead.
items.content: Two people were killed and 14 injured, with the suspected gunman, identified by some media outlets, also shot dead.

clusters.title: India and Canada strike 'landmark' uranium supply deal
clusters.neutral_headline: India and Canada reset ties with 'landmark' nuclear energy deal
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, BBC World

items.title: India and Canada strike 'landmark' uranium supply deal
items.standfirst: India and Canada on Monday agreed to a number of key trade accords that included cooperation on critical minerals and a "landmark" uranium supply deal for nuclear power, the countries' leaders said in New Delhi.
items.content: India and Canada on Monday agreed to a number of key trade accords that included cooperation on critical minerals and a "landmark" uranium supply deal for nuclear power, the countries' leaders said in New Delhi.

items.title: India and Canada reset ties with 'landmark' nuclear energy deal
items.standfirst: A host of deals, including one to supply India with uranium, is unveiled as Mark Carney meets Narendra Modi in Delhi.
items.content: A host of deals, including one to supply India with uranium, is unveiled as Mark Carney meets Narendra Modi in Delhi.
